Class SkeletonPane

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
com.dlsc.gemsfx.SkeletonPane
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
@DefaultProperty("content") public class SkeletonPane extends javafx.scene.layout.Region
Two-slot container that swaps between a skeleton placeholder and the real content based on loading.

The pane hosts non-null slots in internal containers and keeps them attached while present. The inactive container is invisible and mouse-transparent. The original slot node's visibility and mouse transparency are not mutated.

The pane's preferred size falls back to the managed real content's preferred size when present, so the pane's layout bounds stay constant across the loading flip. If no managed content is present, the managed skeleton drives preferred size instead.

    Type
    Property
    Description
    final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node>
    content
    Real content shown when loading is false.
    final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty
    loading
    Whether the pane is currently in the loading state.
    final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node>
    skeleton
    Placeholder node shown while loading is true.

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SkeletonPane()
    Creates an empty pane with loading set to true.

    Creates a pane preloaded with the given skeleton and content, starting in the loading state.

    Creates a pane with explicit initial slots and loading flag.

  • Property Details

    • skeleton

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> skeletonProperty
      Placeholder node shown while loading is true. May be null.
      See Also:
      • getSkeleton()
      • setSkeleton(Node)
      • skeletonProperty()

    • content

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> contentProperty
      Real content shown when loading is false. May be null.
      See Also:
      • getContent()
      • setContent(Node)
      • contentProperty()

    • loading

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty loadingProperty
      Whether the pane is currently in the loading state. When true, the skeleton slot is active; when false, the content slot is active.
      See Also:
      • isLoading()
      • setLoading(boolean)
      • loadingProperty()

  • Constructor Details

    • SkeletonPane

      public SkeletonPane()
      Creates an empty pane with loading set to true.

    • SkeletonPane

      public SkeletonPane(javafx.scene.Node skeleton, javafx.scene.Node content)
      Creates a pane preloaded with the given skeleton and content, starting in the loading state.
      Parameters:
      skeleton - placeholder shown while loading; may be null
      content - real content shown when loaded; may be null

    • SkeletonPane

      public SkeletonPane(javafx.scene.Node skeleton, javafx.scene.Node content, boolean loading)
      Creates a pane with explicit initial slots and loading flag.
      Parameters:
      skeleton - placeholder; may be null
      content - real content; may be null
      loading - initial loading state
